free_text: Spectroscopic study of P-31 and S-32 by the (d,n)
reaction at E(d)=7 MeV.
Six NE 213 liquid scintillator detectors were
used. For the Si-30(d,n) experiment the scintillators
were 10.2 cm in diameter and 2.5 cm in thickness.
For the P-31(d,n) experiment the scintillators
were 10.2 cm in diameter and 5.1 cm in thickness.
EN Tandem Van de Graaff generator
DWBA and the Hauser-Feshbach formalism.
Neutron time-of-flight spectrum from the Si-30(d,n) and
the P-31(d,n) reactions were observed.
.By CAJAD.
.The total uncertainty in absolute
cross sections lies between 15% and 25% and is due
primarily to statistical considerations.
.Statistical error is reported by authors on
figure.
.Digitizing error of angle.
.Digitizing error of data.
SB
Figure 2-3
The absolute differential cross sections were corrected
by about 15% for neutron absorption in the wall of the
scattering chamber and in the air along the flight
path.
The Si-30 target consisted of a layers of 95 mug/cm**2
SiO(2) enriched to 95.5% in Si-30 on a 0.5 mm gold
backing.
.The uncertainty is measured by authors,
except E-LVL=1268, 2226 keV it is E-LVL-ERR=10 keV,
E-LVL=3125 keV it is E-LVL-ERR=8 keV,
E-LVL=7213 keV it is E-LVL-ERR=5 keV,
E-LVL=8564 keV it is E-LVL-ERR=7 keV.
Figure 2.

.The uncertainty is measured by authors.
Figure 5,6,7
The P-31 target was prepared by evaporation of red
phosphorus on a 0.5 mm gold backing. The target
thickness was about 190 mug/cm**2.
.The uncertainty is measured by authors.
